commit.message:
fix: remove invalid :-0 default syntax inside ${#arr[@]} in _tui_status_bar

${#TX_LOCK[@]:-0} is not valid bash — the :- default operator cannot be
used inside the array-length ${#...} form. ${#arr[@]} already returns 0
for an empty or unset array, so the default is redundant and causes a
'bad substitution' error on TUI startup.

commit.message:
docs: add diagnostic matrix to spec training method page

Why: After spec11 r1 finished with 2/35 exact match, operators need a
concrete symptom→cause→action matrix to decide the next move without
reasoning from first principles. The existing page had methodology and
decision cards but no operational lookup table.

Added three new sections:
- Diagnostic Matrix: 10-row table mapping run symptoms (low exact across
  all layouts, probe accounting bugs, loss plateau, stage label bugs)
  to likely causes and concrete actions, with real examples from spec03
  through spec11 r1
- Reading the Probe Report: 5 metrics with healthy ranges and red flags
  (exact_match, renderable, rendered_svg_ok, per-layout, train/dev/test)
- Reading the Loss Curve: 6 patterns with interpretations (smooth descent,
  plateau, stage boundary spike, parity divergence, grad norm collapse,
  oscillation)

These complement the training-intuition page's Phase 1-7 matrix, which
covers training dynamics (gradients, attention, weights). This new matrix
covers experiment-level decisions (probes, curriculum, representation).

Test: Site builds cleanly. All tables balanced (9/9). Cross-reference
to training-intuition.html updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: harden IR hub and visualizer against DOM/escaping issues

Why: Code review audit found two robustness gaps outside the dataset
viewer: (1) open_ir_visualizer.py injected model_name into <title>
without HTML-escaping, (2) open_ir_hub.py used 18 getElementById
calls with no null guards, so bind() would crash if any element was
missing in a degraded environment.

Changes:
- IR visualizer: escape model_name via html.escape() in <title> tag
- IR hub: wrap _els in a Proxy that returns a no-op stub for missing
  elements (graceful degradation, no silent crashes)
- IR hub: bind() now uses onEl() helper that skips missing elements

Test: L1=170 L2=109 L3=56 (335 checks, all green). Hub JS syntax
verified via node --check. Hub regenerated with 45 runs.

commit.message:
feat: synthesize vocabulary/classification from structured-atoms catalogs

Structured-atoms workspaces (spec06+) store training data as DSL text
in render_catalog.json, not as raw SVG files.  The Vocabulary, Browse,
Classification, and Quality tabs were empty because they expected
normalized_assets_manifest.json which doesn't exist for this pipeline.

Added _synthesize_structured_atoms() that detects render catalogs and
synthesizes equivalent normalized (DSL tag frequency, entry counts) and
classified (layout as family, topic as source) data so all tabs populate
without changing the rendering code.

Also fixed script name: normalize_svg_dataset_v7.py → normalize_svg_assets_v7.py
in empty-state operator commands.

Why: Operators saw blank Vocabulary/Classification/Browse/Quality tabs for
all spec06-08 runs despite having 1800 training entries with rich metadata.

Test: L1 167 + L2 109 + L3 50 = 326 checks pass. All 11 spec06-08
viewers regenerated with synthesized data (1800 entries, 30-38 tag types).

fix: use concrete paths in dataset viewer empty-state commands

Replace <DATASET_DIR> and <RUN_DIR> placeholders with runtime ws and
runDir variables so operators see copy-paste-ready commands with the
actual run directory embedded. Add user-select:all to command code
elements for easy copying.

Why: Operators had to manually figure out paths when tabs showed empty
state messages. Now the commands contain the real dataset/run paths.

rendered blank when their data sources (normalized corpus, classification

Changes:
- Added emptyTabHtml() helper: icon + title + reason + numbered operator

- Vocabulary: shows normalize command when no tag_totals exist
- Classification: shows classifier command when no classEntries
- Browse: shows classifier command when no assets to browse
- Candidates: shows classifier command when no split candidates

Each empty state tells the operator exactly which script to run.

feat: add Training Dynamics tab and cross-run comparison

Why: Training telemetry (loss curves, gradient norms, LR schedule, step
timing) existed in JSON files but was never visualized. The IR hub showed
final_loss as a scalar but had no cross-run comparison capability.

Dataset Viewer — Training Dynamics tab (📈):
- _load_training_data(): reads loss curve, grad norms, step profile
- Downsamples to ~500 pts via stride-based sampling (3MB → ~200KB)
- Canvas-based chart engine (drawCanvasChart) — zero external deps
- 5 charts: loss CK vs PT, parity drift, LR schedule, grad norms, timing
- Stage breakdown table with per-stage loss reduction
- CK_TRAINING embedded data variable with summary metrics
- renderTraining() in renderAll() pipeline

IR Hub — Cross-Run Comparison:
- _extract_loss_curve_summary(): start/final loss, convergence rate,
  reduction ratio, downsampled 50-pt sparkline per run
- loss_curve_summary field in RunRecord dataclass
- Loss Curve Overlay canvas (drawHubLossOverlay) in compare panel
- Training Surface compare section: start loss, reduction, convergence
  rate, total steps, final LR columns
- Color-coded per-run sparklines with legend

Test Coverage:
- Contract: 13 tabs (was 12), 45 required functions (was 42)
- L2: fmtAxisVal test vectors (7 cases) in ds_pure_functions.js
- L3: 50 checks (was 44) — panel-training, renderTraining,
  drawCanvasChart, CK_TRAINING, drawHubLossOverlay, loss_curve_summary
- L1+L2+L3: 326 total checks all passing

Test: make v7-visualizer-health (167 L1 + 109 L2 pass)

commit.message:
feat: add python-backend/ — reorganised FastAPI service with physics engine

Introduces a clean python-backend/ directory that reorganises the existing
backend/ Python FastAPI service with a more modular package layout.

Structure
- app/main.py           FastAPI app; CORS reads ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var,
                        lifespan handles DB init/shutdown, /health endpoint
- app/physics/engine.py Standalone physics engine with real formulas:
                          calculate_transformer_loss (IEEE two-part model)
                          calculate_line_loss (I²R Joule heating)
                          analyze_energy_balance (full residual analysis)
                          classify_balance_status
- app/api/v1/analysis.py POST /analyze, GET /history, GET /{id}
- app/api/v1/grid.py    GET /topology, /regions, /substations
- app/api/v1/stream.py  SSE /events/{substation_id} + POST /ingest
- app/api/v1/ai.py      POST /interpret/{id}, GET /status, GET /ghi/dashboard
- app/schemas/models.py All Pydantic v2 request/response models
- app/db/config.py      Async SQLAlchemy; Supabase/SQLite detection;
                        SQLite-safe version query
- app/middleware/auth.py JWT Bearer dependency; raises at startup if
                        JWT_SECRET_KEY is default in production
- app/ai/claude_service.py Claude/OpenAI wrapper with offline fallback
- app/utils/helpers.py  Utility functions
- app/tests/test_physics.py  31 pytest tests (all passing)
- alembic/              Async migration env + script template
- requirements.txt, Dockerfile, .env.example, pytest.ini, alembic.ini

Also fixes nodejs-backend default PYTHON_BACKEND_URL to port 8001 to
match the python-backend default port.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
